events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: the misread sight-glass bubbles were used as the sole basis for judging charge adequacy, but bubbles alone are an unreliable indicator (flash gas, non-condensables, or metering-device behavior can cause false readings) — the manufacturer charging chart requires comparing measured superheat/subcooling to chart values, which was never done. This makes the charge determination unverified and the diagnostic step defective, so I marked it **fail**, risk **high**, condition **installed-defective**, with rework actions to redo the charge check via superheat/subcooling against the mfr chart.
